generalities.text: Main THE ENCYCLOPEDIA OF PURE MATERIA MEDICA By TIMOTHY F. ALLEN, A.M., M.D. Presented by Médi-T FERRUM SULFURICUM. Ferrous sulphate; SO 3 FeO 7 H 2 O, Iron or green vitriol, Copperas. Preparation , Trituration with sugar of milk. Authority. O. A. Moore, effects on a boy aged 14 years, of picking, for three days, the crystals from strings, in a vessel where it was placed to crystallize; Lond. Med. Gaz., N. S. (1841-2), 2, 231. ► Headache. ► Sickness, which continued for two hours, during which he vomited two or three times. ► Colicky pains in abdomen. ► Contractions in arms, hands, and feet. Arms bent at elbows, and fingers drawn towards forearm by flexure of wrist. ► Knees slightly flexed; toes drawn upwards rigidly towards ankle. Hands yield to forcible extension, but immediately return to same condition. ► Limbs very painful, especially the feet and calves, where the pain was "distracting." SUPPLEMENT: FERRUM SULFURICUM. Authority. 2 , M. P. Limousin, Lamothe, Annal. d'Hyg., Jan. 1851 (Pharm. Journ., vol. xi, 1851-52, p. 84), a man named Matet was poisoned by an unknown quantity. ► Groaning, [2] . ► Vomiting, [2] . ► Complained of a pain in his side, [2] . Copyright © Médi-T ® 2000 Main
